summary: Regional census from the Nazca Valley (Santa Clara) using a 3-color encoding system: AB=geographic zones (WHERE, 47%), MB=administrative categories (WHAT, 34%), HB=production/identity (WHO/HOW MUCH, 17%). 369 cords, 40 clusters = ~40 geographic zones. First word kama (govern) = thesis, last word tata (father) = from state to individual. The reference model for multi-color encoding in the khipu corpus. Date: Mode B checkbox month 7. Ethnologisches Museum Berlin. 22 STRING, 73% coverage.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  369 cords (L1: 257, L2: 109, L3: 3). Architecture: 0-SEUL, 40 clusters.
  3-COLOR CENSUS SYSTEM: AB(174), MB(125), HB(61).
  Provenance: Santa Clara, Nazca — southern coast, Aymara zone.
  Museum: Ethnologisches Museum, Berlin.
  Date: Mode B checkbox month 7.
  22 STRING (73% hits), 278 INT, 69 EMPTY (19%).

FIRST WORD / LAST WORD
  First word (C7, MB): kama = 'to govern' — the thesis. Census is about GOVERNANCE.
  Last word (C255, AB): tata = 'father' — closing entry. From governance (the system) to the individual (the household head).

HYPOTHESIS
  UR198 is a regional census using 3-color encoding where each color = one dimension. The 40 clusters represent ~40 geographic zones, each with multi-colored entries tracking households, categories, and production. The reference model for multi-color encoding.

COLOR-AS-ROLE — 3-DIMENSIONAL CENSUS
  AB (174 cords, 47%): GEOGRAPHIC LAYER — where. Words: qaqa(rock)x2, qaki x2, maqa x2, chiqa(truth), pama(formula), taqa(boundary), chaki(foot).
  MB (125 cords, 34%): CATEGORY LAYER — what. Words: kama(govern), qaqa(rock), kaqa(wall), kiki(self), tama(herd), taqa(boundary).
  HB (61 cords, 17%): PRODUCTION/IDENTITY LAYER — who/how much. Words: qaki x2, piqa(who?).
  Cross-color actors: qaki (AB+HB = terrain feature AND production unit), qaqa (AB+MB = landmark AND administrative boundary), taqa (AB+MB = geographic AND administrative boundary).
  3-color system creates a 3-column spreadsheet: each row (cluster) has cells in 3 dimensions.

KEY VOCABULARY
  qaki (zone identifier x4, AB+HB)
  qaqa (rock/terrain x3, AB+MB)
  maqa (boundary marker x2, AB)
  taqa (administrative boundary x2, AB+MB)
  kama (to govern x1, MB), piqa (who? x1, HB), chiqa (truth/verification x1, AB)
  kaqa (wall-AY x1, MB), pama (formula x1, AB), qama (to inhabit x1)
  chaki (foot x1), kiki (self x1, MB), tama (herd x1, MB), kaki (corn-AY x1), tata (father x1, AB)

STRUCTURE — 40 GEOGRAPHIC ZONES
  40 clusters of ~6 cords each (257 L1 / 40 = 6.4). Each cluster = one zone with:
    AB cords = geographic description
    MB cords = administrative category
    HB cords = production count

CROSS-CORPUS
  UR198 vs UR113 'Valhalla' (both Nazca): UR198 = 3 colors (3 dimensions), UR113 = 21 colors (~21 dimensions). Different complexity, same region.
  The 3-color model as reference: simplest multi-color encoding. Others use more: UR203 (10), UR110 (7), UR113 (21), UR144 (2).
  All follow same principle: color = data dimension. UR198 is the clearest demonstration.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — 3-color census system, multi-color encoding reference model
Cross-corpus: UR113 (Nazca audit, 21 colors), UR203 (ceque registry, 10 colors)
Nazca Valley — southern coast Aymara administrative zone
